steps.title: Connect color to sediment
steps.content: The conclusion compares Missouri River segments flowing into lakes with segments flowing out of lakes. The passage says sediment-rich rivers appear yellow, and the researchers classify wavelengths of 560 nm or above as yellow. So supporting data should show inflow segments at or above that threshold and outflow segments below it.

steps.title: Compare inflow and outflow
steps.content: D gives exactly that comparison: inflow segments usually have wavelengths above 560 nm, while outflow segments usually have wavelengths below 560 nm. A concerns chlorophyll instead of sediment and does not compare inflow with outflow. B describes lake shores and centers. C says most segments are above 560 nm but gives no inflow-outflow contrast. The answer is D.
